publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday September 26, 2020 at 03:21

did.unittitle: Western postcard collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Thomas C. Klopp on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund, William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3, and the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2011-2014.
July 1998 Acquisition: purchased from Ronald Rayman on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund.
November 2004 Acquisition: purchased from Sindell and Company on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana.
May 2009 Acquisition: purchased from Ray C. Chasteen on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund.
October 2010 Acquisition: purchased from Ronald Rayman on the William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3.
December 2010 Acquisition: gift of George A. Miles.
April 2011 Acquisition: gift of Michael Vinson.
May 2011 Acquisition: gift of Peter Sindell.
July 2011 Acquisition: purchased from Thomas C. Klopp on the Ramona Lazare Fund.
August 2011 Acquisitions: purchased from Thomas C. Klopp on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund and Ramona Lazare Fund.
September 2011 Acquisitions: purchased from Thomas C. Klopp on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund.
October 2011 Acquisitions: purchased from Thomas C. Klopp on the Winlock William Miller, Jr. Memorial Fund and William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3.
August 2012 Acquisition: purchased from Vigilante Rare Documents on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana.
July 2014 Acquisition: source unknown.
October 2015 Acquisition: purchased from Michael Maslan on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into twenty-one groupings: July 1998 Acquisition; May 2009 Acquisition; October 2010 Acquisition; December 2010 Acquisition; April 2011 Acquisition; May 2011 Acquisition; July 2011 Acquisition; August 2011 Acquisitions; September 2011 Acquisitions; October 2011 Acquisitions; November 2011 Acquisition; December 2011 Acquisitions; January 2012 Acquisitions; February 2012 Acquisitions; March 2012 Acquisitions; April 2012 Acquisition; August 2012 Acquisition; March 2014 Acquisitions; April 2014 Acquisition; July 2014 Acquisition; and October 2015 Acquisition.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Western Postcard Collection. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Western Postcard Collection consists of picture postcards depicting locations and scenes of the American West.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Western Postcard Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Black and white photographic postcards of scenes of Sante Fe, New Mexico

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Removed from: Brother David, Interesting guide to see the oldest church, the oldest house, and the oldest bell : all in Santa Fe, with some historical items about New Mexico (Santa Fe: St. Michael's College, circa 1908) (call number Zc54 908da).

did.unittitle: Color photographic postcard of Chilkoot Pass, Alaska by Portland Post Card Company
Black and white photographic postcard of Steamer "Lavelle Young" landing at Fairbanks, Alaska by P. E. J. Cern
Black and white photographic postcard of Seattle, Washington by Lowman & Hanford Stationary & Printing Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 2190. Alaska sailing day, Seattle, Washington.

did.unittitle: Color photographic postcard of gold from Alaska by Portland Post Card Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 9084. One & one half tons of gold from Alaska.

did.unittitle: Color photographic postcard of a gold nugget from Alaska by an unidentified photographer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alaska's largest nugget, weight 182 oz.s, found on No. 5 Anvil Creek.

did.unittitle: Color photographic postcard of the Northern Navigation Company's Yukon River packet Sarah by an unidentified photographer
Color photographic postcard of Chilkoot Pass, Alaska by E. G. Hegg

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Over Chikoot Pass during the Gold Rush in Alaska. Thousands...." Published by Lawman & Hanford.

did.unittitle: Color photographic postcards of scenes of Colorado, Iowa, and South Dakota
Black and white photographic postcard of Skagway, Alaska by Van Ness

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Packer Jack Newman, 5 miles from Skagway-1898.

did.unittitle: Photographic postcards of Albany, Oregon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains black and white photographic postcards, primarily of Albany, Oregon, most likely taken by William F. Peacock.
